json mélange/melange.description: [Traduction en cours] Shuffles the contents of a list into random order.

json ajoute.description: [Traduction en cours] This adds an element to a JSON array.

json supprime.syntax: json supprime propriété/propriete {name} depuis/de {object}%0ajson supprime élément/element {index} depuis/de {array}
json supprime.description: [Traduction en cours] This deletes the named property, or the element at a specific index, from the JSON array given.
json supprime.examples: json supprime propriété/propriete `name` depuis PersonalDetails%0ajson supprime élément/element 5 de TheArray

json format.description: [Traduction en cours] ~m:json format~ deals with badly-formatted JSON strings that contain redundant spacing and line breaks. It uses the internal ~m:JSON.parse~ and ~m:JSON.stringify~ to get the item into a standard format.

json analyse.description:
[Traduction en cours] This parses a URL, splitting it up into its component parts so they can be easily worked on. In the example above these parts are:%0a%0a~m:protocol~: The protocol: ~m:https://~%0a%0a~m:domain~: The domain: ~m:allspeak.software~%0a%0a~m:path~: The path: ~demos/first~%0a%0a~m:arg~: The CGI parameters: ~value=56~%0a%0aHere, the result in ~m:ParsedValue~ would be%0a%0a~m:{
~%0a~m:  "protocol":"https://",
~%0a~m:  "domain":"allspeak.software",
~%0a~m:  "path":"demos/first",
~%0a~m:  "arg":"value=56"
~%0a~m:}~%0a%0aSee also ~l:history|k|browser!history~ for managing the browser history

json renomme.description: [Traduction en cours] This renames a property in a JSON object.

json trie.description: [Traduction en cours] Sorts the contents of a list into ascending alphabetical order.

json scinde.description: [Traduction en cours] Splits a variable into an array of parts on the given separator. If no separator is given the separator is set to a newline (%0a).
json scinde.examples: json scinde Paragraph dans Lines%0ajson scinde Text sur `~` dans Tokens

json remplace.description: [Traduction en cours] Replaces the element at the given index in a JSON array held in a ~l:variable~ with a new value. The array is parsed, the element replaced, and the result stored back into the variable.
json remplace.examples: json remplace élément/element 2 de Items avec `new value`%0ajson remplace élément/element N de MyArray par Replacement

json clés/cles.description: [Traduction en cours] Returns the keys of all the properties in the JSON object as a JSON array; by default sorted alphabetically. If ~m:unsorted~ is given the list will be unsorted.
json clés/cles.examples: mets le/la/les/l json clés/cles de PersonalDetails dans MyArray%0amets le/la/les/l json clés/cles nontrié/nontrie de List dans Keys

json compte.description: [Traduction en cours] Returns the number of elements in the specified array. See also ~l:json size~, which does the same thing.
json compte.examples: mets le/la/les/l json compte de Rows dans NRows

json taille.description: [Traduction en cours] Returns the number of elements in the specified array. See also ~l:json count~, which does the same thing.
json taille.examples: mets le/la/les/l json taille de Rows dans NRows

json index.description: [Traduction en cours] Returns the zero-based index of the element in the array, or -1 if it is not present.
json index.examples: mets le/la/les/l json index de `August` dans Months dans Index
